title: lupinaster pentaphyllus subsp. angustifolius
intro: Lupinaster pentaphyllus subsp. angustifolius, commonly known as the narrow-leaved or narrow-leafed shrubby lupin, is a plant species belonging to the legume family. This plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds, which contribute to its potential health benefits. The narrow-leaved shrubby lupin has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, colds, and respiratory issues, due to its expectorant and bronchodilator properties. Herbal preparations of this plant, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, have been utilized in folk medicine to promote overall well-being and alleviate symptoms associated with various health conditions.
